summary.intent: Implement 5 Codex pass-5 review findings for the FileUpload component in a Rust UI library (ars-ui), treating them as genuine improvements rather than spec-defensive pushbacks, and fix a resulting branch-coverage CI failure.
summary.outcome: All 5 findings implemented (init state derivation, directory/multiple bypass, drag-during-upload, auto-retry, controlled-mode change callback), spec updated across core and adapter specs, and ars-components branch coverage restored from 89.9% to 90.03% via ~20 targeted coverage tests.

repo: Controlled components follow a strict convention: Props carry `on_X_change: Option<Callback<...>>`, a unit `Effect::XChange` variant is emitted on mutation, and adapters read the new value from the API to invoke the callback — mirroring checkbox/switch exactly.
PendingEffect uses FnOnce closures, so non-Copy types like Vec<Item> can be moved into effect payloads (unlike checkbox's Copy bool).

CI branch coverage uses `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace`; local isolated `-p ars-components` under-counts because cross-crate tests in the workspace also exercise ars-components branches.
Coverage threshold check is `branch_pct >= 90.0` (inclusive); CI measures are always higher than isolated local runs.
ars-components default features are `["std"]` (no i18n), but CI's --workspace run unifies features via a member that enables `ars-i18n/icu4x`, so ars-components effectively gets i18n in CI.
Both core component specs (`spec/components/`) and adapter specs (`spec/leptos-components/`, `spec/dioxus-components/`) must be kept in sync whenever new Props or Effects are added.
Snapshots use `insta`; accepted via `cargo insta accept`.
The spec models `RetryFile` as reset-only (Failed→Pending) and `DragEnter` from Idle-only — both were deliberate design choices that pass-5 correctly identified as suboptimal.

code.finding: `init` was returning Idle unconditionally, violating the State::Uploading invariant when seeded with uploading files. Fixed to derive state from the seeded file list.
`validate_files` had an impl-added `!multiple` single-file guard that was never in the spec; directory mode should bypass it since selecting a folder implies multiple-file accept.
New `commit_files` helper centralizes all file writes and performs optimistic controlled-sync, ensuring `api.files()` always reflects the queue in both controlled and uncontrolled modes.
`RetryFile` arm now chains `StartUpload` when `auto_upload` is true, mirroring `FilesSelected`'s behavior.

Coverage tests must use `StrongSend: Arc::new(|_| {})` convention (same as checkbox tests); `let _ =` on `AttrMap` triggers clippy because it has a destructor — use explicit drops or avoid the binding.

workflow: Later Codex review passes (5+) tend to produce design-expansion findings rather than clear spec bugs; evaluating each against the spec before implementing saves wasted effort.

When CI branch coverage fails, generate lcov with `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch -p <crate> --features <needed>`, then filter BRDA records with count=0 to find uncovered branches by line number.
Running the definitive `--workspace` coverage as a background task (~12 min) before committing avoids a second CI failure from thin margin.
Spec changes should be treated as first-class deliverables alongside code — both core and adapter specs need updating for any new Props/Effects.

friction: hgT's `init` change invalidated the previously-committed `SetFiles(None)` test setup, requiring a rework mid-batch.
Local isolated coverage measurement consistently underestimates CI's --workspace figure, making it difficult to confirm a fix without the slow full-workspace run.
41 uncovered branches introduced by passes 4+5 required ~20 new tests across multiple edge cases to close the gap.
The background --workspace coverage run took ~12 minutes, blocking the commit.
Clippy flagged `let _ =` bindings on `AttrMap` (has destructor) in coverage tests, requiring a cleanup pass.
i18n feature gate caused 14 test failures in local coverage runs, requiring explicit `--features i18n` to get a valid measurement.
open_items: Adapter implementations (Leptos, Dioxus) need to wire the new `on_files_change` Prop and `FilesChanged` Effect per the updated adapter specs — currently only the specs were updated, not the adapter code.
Finding-6 pushback thread was still open after pass 5 — not addressed in this session.
The single remaining near-dead uncovered branch (`normalized == ".png"` literal comparison) may indicate dead code worth removing from the accept-pattern normalization logic.

The controlled-mode change from Bindable-reactivity-only to Bindable+callback is an architectural shift; the adapters' controlled-sync documentation now differs from the actual Bindable-only implementation until adapters are updated.
